originalTitle: Iran war cost: Average U.S. household paying $450 more on gas and energy
summary: Higher energy costs can force consumers to raid their savings and lean more on debt to cover expenses.
content:
Americans have spent nearly $450 extra per household on rising energy costs during the Iran war , according to an analysis shared exclusively with CNBC's Steve Liesman.

The average household has shelled out $447.19 for additional fuel-related expenses since the conflict began on Feb. 28, data from Moody's Analytics found. That's cumulatively cost American consumers nearly $60 billion as gas prices and airline fares have surged.

Moody's data puts a dollar amount on a portion of the economic pain Americans are feeling as the war reaches its three-month mark. Higher energy costs can force consumers to raid their savings and lean more on debt to cover expenses.

"Unless the war ends soon, financially pressed consumers will have no option but to turn more cautious in their spending, threatening the already soft economy," said Mark Zandi , Moody's chief economist.

If prices stay at current levels, the average household could take a hit of almost $2,000 at the one-year mark of the war, Zandi said.

Roughly half of the increased energy spending so far comes from higher gasoline prices. The average unleaded gallon in the U.S. cost about $4.39 on Friday, up more than 47% since the start of March, according to AAA .

Pricier diesel, which is used in vehicles like delivery trucks and boats, has resulted in more than $20 billion in additional expenses for consumers. The price of diesel has similarly jumped roughly 47% since the beginning of March to around $5.52 a gallon, per AAA.

Consumers have given up nearly $10 billion as a result of rising costs for jet fuel. Airline fares climbed more than 20% in April compared with 12 months ago, federal government inflation data shows.

That nearly $450 impact more than erased the boost of $384 per household from bigger tax returns this year under President Donald Trump 's "big, beautiful bill," according to Moody's. Most of the benefits from larger tax cuts have already been exhausted, Zandi said.

Goldman Sachs said it expects higher energy prices to "erode" consumers' spending power through the rest of 2026. It should specifically hamper lower-income households that spend a larger percentage of budgets on food and energy, the bank said.

Costco saw "record-breaking" gas volumes at the end of its fiscal quarter as drivers sought out its lower-priced fuel, the wholesaler said Thursday. McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski warned this month that consumer spending — specifically among lower-income cohorts — "may be getting a little bit worse" as energy prices pinch pocketbooks.

Consumer spending rose 0.5% from March to April, according to government figures released Thursday . But other data points show that isn't necessarily coming from discretionary funds.

Income growth came in flat for April, missing the consensus forecast among economists for a 0.4% increase.

The personal savings rate fell to 2.6% in April, one of the lowest readings since the global financial crisis. It's far off highs above 31% seen in 2020, signaling that consumers have continued to spend through pandemic stimulus and rainy-day stashes amid inflationary pressures.

American credit card debt came in at $1.25 trillion in the first quarter, up close to 6% from a year ago, the New York Federal Reserve said this month. That's near the all-time record set at the end of 2025.

"Consumers are increasingly facing an income squeeze, which is forcing them to use savings, credit and wealth to sustain their spending patterns," said Gregory Daco, chief economist at EY-Parthenon. "What we're seeing is, essentially, the use of savings to offset weak income growth."

— CNBC's Steve Liesman and Betsy Spring contributed to this report.
chineseTitle: 伊朗战争成本：美国家庭平均在天然气和能源上多支付 450 美元
chineseSummary: 能源成本上升可能迫使消费者动用储蓄，更多地依靠债务来支付开支。
chineseContent:
根据与 CNBC 的 Steve Liesman 独家分享的一项分析，在伊朗战争期间，美国人每户因能源成本上涨而多花费了近 450 美元。

穆迪分析的数据显示，自 2 月 28 日冲突开始以来，平均家庭已支付 447.19 美元用于额外的燃料相关费用。随着汽油价格和机票价格的飙升，美国消费者累计损失了近 600 亿美元。

穆迪的数据对战争进入三个月大关时美国人所感受到的部分经济痛苦进行了估算。能源成本上升可能迫使消费者动用储蓄，更多地依靠债务来支付开支。

穆迪首席经济学家马克·赞迪表示：“除非战争尽快结束，否则经济拮据的消费者将别无选择，只能在支出上更加谨慎，从而威胁到本已疲软的经济。”

赞迪说，如果价格保持在目前的水平，那么在战争结束一年后，普通家庭可能会遭受近 2,000 美元的损失。

迄今为止增加的能源支出大约有一半来自汽油价格上涨。据 AAA 称，周五美国无铅加仑的平均价格约为 4.39 美元，自 3 月初以来上涨了 47% 以上。

用于运输卡车和船只等车辆的柴油价格较高，给消费者带来了超过 200 亿美元的额外支出。根据 AAA，自 3 月初以来，柴油价格同样上涨了约 47%，达到每加仑 5.52 美元左右。

由于航空燃油成本上涨，消费者已经放弃了近 100 亿美元。联邦政府通胀数据显示，4 月份机票价格较 12 个月前上涨了 20% 以上。

穆迪表示，根据唐纳德·特朗普总统的“大而美丽的法案”，今年每户纳税申报单增加了 384 美元，这近 450 美元的影响远远抵消了。赞迪表示，大规模减税带来的大部分好处已经用尽。

高盛表示，预计能源价格上涨将在 2026 年剩余时间内“削弱”消费者的消费能力。该行表示，这将特别阻碍在食品和能源上花费较大比例预算的低收入家庭。

批发商好市多 (Costco) 周四表示，由于司机寻求价格较低的燃料，该公司在其财政季度末的天然气量创下了“破纪录”的纪录。麦当劳首席执行官克里斯·肯普钦斯基(Chris Kempczinski)本月警告称，随着能源价格挤压钱包，消费者支出——特别是低收入群体的支出——“可能会变得更糟”。

根据周四公布的政府数据，3 月至 4 月消费者支出增长了 0.5%。但其他数据点表明，这不一定来自可自由支配的基金。

4 月份收入增长持平，未达到经济学家一致预测的增长 0.4%。

4月份个人储蓄率降至2.6%，是全球金融危机以来的最低读数之一。这一数字与 2020 年 31% 以上的高位相去甚远，这表明消费者在通胀压力下继续通过大流行刺激和未雨绸缪的方式进行支出。

纽约联储本月表示，第一季度美国信用卡债务达 1.25 万亿美元，同比增长近 6%。这接近 2025 年底创下的历史纪录。

安永-帕特农银行首席经济学家格雷戈里·达科表示：“消费者日益面临收入紧缩，这迫使他们利用储蓄、信贷和财富来维持消费模式。” “我们所看到的本质上是利用储蓄来抵消收入增长疲弱的影响。”

— CNBC 的 Steve Liesman 和 Betsy Spring 对本报告做出了贡献。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
